name: Elbmesh Event-Sourced Resource Runtime
summary: Current Elbmesh core runtime shape for executing an Action against one replayed Resource and appending typed Resource Events with journaled execution outcomes.

rules: Implement Resource behavior through explicit Resource, Action, Event, Apply, and Handle traits.
Use ActionContext to record typed Events for the Action's target Resource.
Let ActionExecutor load Resource history, replay through apply_recorded, run Handle<Action>, append pending Events, and return an ActionReceipt.
Journal ActionCalled, ActionCompleted, ActionRejected, and ActionFailed records when an Action Journal is configured.
constraints: Do not add cross-Resource mutation inside a single Action handler.
Do not bypass expected-version appends when adding Resource Events.
Do not turn execution journal records into Resource replay inputs.
checks: The Action implementation names exactly one Resource type through Action::Resource.
Event implementations name their owner Resource through Event::Resource.
Apply implementations are explicit for each supported Event type.
Handler domain errors use typed ActionFailure codes.

items: Resource defines a RESOURCE_TYPE and apply_recorded replay entry point.
Action and Event each bind to a Resource and carry ACTION_TYPE or EVENT_TYPE plus schema identity/version metadata.
Apply<Event> mutates reconstructed Resource state from a typed Event.
Handle<Action> decides whether to record Events or return a typed domain/runtime error.
ActionExecutor loads history, sorts by sequence, replays it, runs the handler, appends pending Resource Events with ExpectedVersion::Exact, and builds an ActionReceipt.

Action Journal support exists for ActionCalled, ActionCompleted, ActionRejected, and ActionFailed records. Operation Journal is a target architecture concept and should not be claimed as implemented unless code exists.

blocks.description: Core Resource, Action, Event, Apply, Handle, and apply_recorded_event traits/helpers.
Action Journal contracts and in-memory implementation.
Current scenario tests for execution, replay, journals, typed errors, and failure paths.

blocks.reason: The runtime architecture implements explicit Elbmesh boundaries.
